_template_notice: TEMPLATE — copy this file to `metadata_map.json` (same directory) and fill it with your own Slack conversation IDs. The real `metadata_map.json` is gitignored so personal names and channel IDs never ship in the repo. Keep the structure below; the slack skill reads these keys.

_how_to_populate.audience: An Agent building the real metadata_map.json from a user's live Slack. This records how the committed real map was produced so it can be regenerated on a new machine.
steps: 1. Load the Slack MCP tool schemas first — they are deferred: ToolSearch("select:mcp__claude_ai_Slack__slack_search_public_and_private,mcp__claude_ai_Slack__slack_read_channel,mcp__claude_ai_Slack__slack_search_users,mcp__claude_ai_Slack__slack_search_channels"). A sub-agent must do this itself; schemas do not inherit from the parent.

2. CRITICAL channel_types gotcha: every search MUST pass channel_types="public_channel,private_channel,mpim,im". The default is public-only and hides 80%+ of conversations (all DMs, group DMs, private channels).

3. Enumerate what the user actually participates in: slack_search_public_and_private(query="from:me after:<~90 days ago>", channel_types="public_channel,private_channel,mpim,im", response_format="detailed"). Use detailed format — it returns the channel_id — and page a few times to cover the tail.

4. Resolve names to IDs as needed: slack_search_channels(query="channel-name") for channels; slack_search_users(query="Person Name") for DM partners. Get your own user_id via slack_search_users(query="me").

5. Classify each hit into the sections below: public/private channels -> channels{} grouped under a sensible category, with name, channel_id, type, topic; group DMs (mpim) -> group_dms[] with participants, channel_id, purpose; 1:1 DMs (im) -> direct_messages[] with person, dm_id, role.
6. Watch for bots that look like people (calendar/notification bots) — do not map them as people; record the ambiguity in special_notes instead. Also note renames and name changes there.
7. Rebuild the quick_reference id arrays from the entries above so they stay in sync.
8. Set metadata.created_date / last_updated / last_verified (Australia/Brisbane date) and metadata.user_id.
9. Save as metadata_map.json in this directory — the gitignored real file. NEVER commit it; only this .example.json is tracked.

metadata.discovery_method: Slack MCP search with keyword and channel_types queries
metadata.scope: All private channels, group DMs, and 1:1 DMs visible in <your> Slack sidebar

<category_b>.name: example-channel
<category_b>.notes: Optional free-text note, e.g. former name or context

group_dms.purpose: What this group DM is for

direct_messages.role: Their role / how you relate to them

special_notes.example_note: Free-text notes about edge cases — name changes, channel renames, bots that look like people, DM-vs-group ambiguity, etc.
